candidate.title: Resolved Biren deployment-envelope fields from local product captures

repo_state.git_status_before_task: git status --short returned no changed paths before planner output; generator status before manifest write showed only task-scoped ai_infra edits.

local_checks.raw_manifest_scan: Searched local compute accelerator raw captures, source registry, structured catalog YAML, Compute Accelerator Crawl Inventory, Compute Accelerator Parameter Comparison, coverage-map.json, loop-state.json, and r9 hardware gap proof for Biren 106B, 106M, 166C, 166L, 166M, form factor, peak power, TDP, compute, memory, host interface, interconnect, DPU, SmartNIC, aggregate, multi-variant, and blocked-source boundaries. Existing local product pages expose form factor and peak power for the selected Biren records, but do not expose compute, memory, host-interface, or interconnect values.
local_checks.wiki_search: Compute Accelerator Parameter Comparison already had raw comparison rows for Biren Bili 106B, 106M, 166C, 166L, and 166M, and the 20260706 baseline reconciliation already classified July Biren refreshes as existing source-profile evidence. The structured catalog YAML did not contain Biren SKUs, observations, or resolved fields before this task, and r9 task 2 resolved only Cambricon MLU370-X4, Cambricon MLU370-X8, and Kunlunxin RG800.
local_checks.domain_index_scan: ai_infra wiki/index.md already routes Compute Accelerator Spec Catalog, Compute Accelerator Parameter Comparison, Compute Accelerator Crawl Inventory, Field Glossary, Spec Sources, and AI Infra Coverage Map. No duplicate curated page was needed; task 2 updates existing catalog and coverage pages only.

duplicate_checks.result: Duplicate boundary: R9 task 2 resolved Cambricon MLU370-X4, Cambricon MLU370-X8, and Kunlunxin RG800. It did not add Biren SKUs, observations, or resolved rows.

The page already records Biren raw comparison facts. This task avoids duplicating raw rows and instead adds structured resolved catalog rows for the already source-backed form-factor and peak-power fields.
The inventory already classifies July Biren captures as refreshes of existing source profiles. No new crawler source, subscription, or external capture is required.
Before this task, the structured catalog had no Biren Bili 106B, 106M, 166C, 166L, or 166M SKU IDs and no matching observation or resolved entries.
Hardware-accelerator remained partial with an unresolved-field gap. The existing state knew the compute accelerator corpus but did not record a resolved Biren deployment-envelope slice.

selected_source_captures.coverage: Product page text exposes a full-height full-length double-wide PCIe card form factor and 300 W peak power. No compute, memory, host-interface, or interconnect fields are promoted.
Product page text exposes an air-cooled OAM module form factor and 400 W peak power. No compute, memory, host-interface, or interconnect fields are promoted.
Product page text exposes a full-height full-length 290 mm double-wide PCIe inference card form factor and 300 W peak power. No compute, memory, host-interface, or interconnect fields are promoted.
Product page text exposes a cold-plate liquid-cooled OAM module form factor and 600 W peak power. No compute, memory, host-interface, or interconnect fields are promoted.
Product page text exposes a 4U OAM V1.1 air-cooled module form factor and 550 W peak power. No compute, memory, host-interface, or interconnect fields are promoted.

blocked_or_deferred_sources.reason: The local Biren captures describe product positioning, form factor, and peak power, but do not expose explicit TFLOPS/TOPS, memory capacity, memory bandwidth, host interface, or interconnect values. These fields remain unresolved.

blocked_or_deferred_sources.record: Huawei Atlas 300I A2, Cambricon MLU370-S4/S8, and Kunlunxin R200/R200-8F
blocked_or_deferred_sources.reason: These local rows remain multi-variant boundaries. Task 2 does not split variants or promote values without explicit per-variant catalog modeling.

Several local DPU and storage-offload captures expose useful values, but the existing structured schema does not model IOPS or latency. This task avoids schema expansion and leaves those fields for a future scoped change.

blocked_or_deferred_sources.record: AWS Trn2, Huawei Atlas 800T A3, Kunlunxin R480-X8, and portfolio/discovery pages
blocked_or_deferred_sources.reason: Cloud offerings, aggregate systems, portfolio pages, and discovery indexes remain outside single-card resolved fields unless an explicit normalization boundary exists.

schema_change.reason: No schema change was required; form_factor and tdp already apply to ai_asic records.

gap_reason: Local product-specific Biren captures already contained unambiguous form-factor and peak-power facts, and the parameter comparison page already recorded them as raw comparison rows, but the normalized catalog lacked Biren SKU, observation, and resolved spec rows. Task 2 fills only that bounded deployment-envelope gap while preserving unresolved boundaries for missing compute/memory fields, multi-variant rows, aggregate systems, schema gaps, and runtime-only evidence.
no_new_crawl_reason: No bounded external capture was needed. The selected product-specific pages were already present in local raw evidence and the source registry already contained matching official source IDs.
